entity.name: EMILIA Protocol, Inc.
entity.description: EMILIA Protocol, Inc. maintains EMILIA Protocol: an open Apache-2.0 protocol and reference toolkit for independently verifying authorization evidence for consequential machine actions. The public-good surface includes JavaScript, Python, and Go reference verifiers, adversarial conformance vectors, formal models, executable security claims, and individual IETF Internet-Drafts. The for-profit company funds and stewards the open core.

projects.name: EMILIA Protocol
projects.description: An open protocol and reference toolkit for producing and independently verifying authorization evidence for consequential machine actions. The fundable public surface includes same-team JavaScript, Python, and Go reference verifiers checked against shared adversarial vectors in CI; the npm packages @emilia-protocol/verify and @emilia-protocol/issue; executable security claims with hashed evidence; formal models with explicit boundedness and exclusions; and the individual Internet-Draft draft-schrock-ep-authorization-receipts-08.

channels.description: GitHub Sponsors (pending enablement for the emiliaprotocol organisation; once active, update this address to https://github.com/sponsors/emiliaprotocol). Until then, contact team@emiliaprotocol.ai to arrange funding.

plans.name: Security hardening of the open verifier toolchain
plans.description: One-time work to harden the open public-good toolchain: cross-language differential and property-based testing across the JavaScript, Python, and Go verifiers; supply-chain hardening (SBOM, provenance, signed releases, reproducible-build check) of the zero-dependency packages; conformance-suite and red-team expansion (PIP-007 escalation path and WebAuthn challenge-binding edge cases); and fuzzing of the parse/verify boundary.

plans.name: Ongoing maintenance
plans.description: Recurring support for ongoing maintenance of the open verifiers, the conformance suite, the formal models, and the IETF Internet-Draft: dependency-free upkeep, CI re-runs of the formal proofs, security triage, and conformance-vector updates.
